mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Steps
mnemonics.content: MAPI - Monitor current resources, Analyze trends and patterns, Plan future capacity needs, Implement solutions

mnemonics.title: Key Capacity Metrics
mnemonics.content: CUPS - CPU utilization, Memory usage, Performance (response time), Storage I/O

mnemonics.title: Scaling Types
mnemonics.content: HAVA - Horizontal (add machines), Vertical (add power to same machine)

mnemonics.title: When to Scale
mnemonics.content: UTPR - Utilization threshold crossed, Performance degrading, Response time increasing, Resource exhaustion imminent

acronyms.usage: Contract defining minimum performance metrics that capacity must support
Reliability metric used to plan redundancy and backup capacity
Primary throughput metric for web servers and APIs
Framework for prioritizing traffic and ensuring consistent performance
Metric to evaluate cost-effectiveness of capacity additions

visualTips.visualization: Think of a car's fuel gauge at 80% - time to plan for next refuel, not wait for empty tank. Red zone starts at 80-90%.
Vertical = adding more floors to a building; Horizontal = building more identical houses side by side.
Chain analogy - system is only as strong as weakest link; identify which component (CPU, disk, network) is limiting throughput.
Weather forecast for IT - like predicting rain, we predict demand using past patterns and current trends.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant manager predicting customer flow - analyze past data, seasonal trends, special events to ensure enough tables, staff, and ingredients.
Traffic light at yellow - warning to prepare for action (red) ahead; 80% utilization means prepare scaling before crisis.
Calling extra staff during holiday shopping - temporary help for temporary peak demand, then release when normal.
Wearing properly fitted clothes - too big wastes fabric (over-provisioning costs), too small is uncomfortable (under-provisioning hurts performance).
